description: You use the elements in the mqtt folder to publish messages to an MQTT server and define the input of MQTT trigger services.

services.name: pub.mqtt:publish
description: WmPublic. Publishes an MQTT message to an MQTT server.

input_parameters.name: connectionAliasName
description: Name of the MQTT connection alias to use to send the MQTT message.

input_parameters.name: topicName
description: Name of the topic to which to publish the message.

input_parameters.name: MqttMessage
description: Document (IData object) A document representing the MQTT message to publish to the MQTT server.

children.name: payload
description: Optional. A document containing the MQTT message payload. Integration Server supports the following formats for the message payload. If you do not specify a payload, the service sends an empty byte[].

children.name: string
description: Optional. Message body in the form of a String.

children.name: bytes
description: Optional. Message body in the form of a one-dimensional byte array.

children.name: qos
description: Specify one of the following:
- 0 At most once. Integration Server publishes the message to the MQTT server once or not at all.
- 1 At least once. Integration Server publishes the message to the MQTT server one or more times. This is the default.
- 2 Exactly once. Integration Server publishes the message to the MQTT server once and only once.

The QoS for publishing messages to the MQTT server is separate from the QoS for receiving messages. As a result, it is possible for a message to be published to the MQTT server with a higher QoS than which the message is received from the MQTT server.

children.name: isRetained
description: Set to:
- true to indicate the message is to be retained by the MQTT server as the last known message for the topic.
- false to indicate the MQTT server will not retain this message as the last known message for the topic. This is the default.

usage_notes: Integration Server relies on Paho to determine the max inflight property value. The max inflight property determines the maximum number of messages that can be published to the MQTT server at one time using the same MQTT connection alias. Paho uses a default of 10. However you can use the watt.server.mqtt.producer.maxInflight server configuration parameter to set the max inflight property value.

services.name: pub.mqtt:triggerSpec
description: WmPublic. Specification for the signature of an MQTT trigger service.

input_parameters.name: topicName
description: Name of the topic from which the message was received. An MQTT trigger can use wildcards to subscribe to multiple topics.

input_parameters.name: MqttMessage
description: Document (IData object) A document representing the MQTT message received by the MQTT trigger.

children.name: payload
description: Optional. A document containing the MQTT message payload. The Decoding Type value for the MQTT trigger determines the format for the message payload passed to the trigger service.

children.name: bytes
description: Optional. MQTT message body in the form of a one-dimensional byte array.

children.name: string
description: Optional. The MQTT message body as a String.

children.name: id
description: Optional. The message ID for the MQTT message which is set only if qos is 1 or 2.

children.name: qos
description: The QoS for a message can be different than the QoS value set for the MQTT trigger. The QoS for publishing messages to the MQTT server is separate from the QoS for receiving messages. When sending the message to the MQTT trigger, the MQTT server uses the lower QoS regardless of whether it was set by the message publisher or by the MQTT trigger.
MQTT version 3.1.1 does not require MQTT servers to support QoS 2. An MQTT server may downgrade a QoS of 2 to 1.

children.name: isRetained
description: - true indicates the MQTT message was retained by the MQTT server as the last known good message.
- false indicates the MQTT message was not retained by the MQTT server as the last known good message.

children.name: isDuplicate
description: Optional. Flag indicating whether the MQTT message is a duplicate of one previously retrieved by the MQTT trigger. The MQTT server resends messages when the MQTT server does not receive an acknowledgment for the message from the MQTT trigger. isDuplicate applies only when the qos is greater than 0.
